message:
Add JSONL evidence validation to BigQuery, Bitbucket, and Dropbox E2E compliance suites

All three connector E2E test files gain an assert_report_logs_validate()
helper that validates the structured JSONL evidence output from E2eReport:

- Verifies report emits non-empty stable JSONL lines
- Validates first line contains deterministic timestamp (epoch zero),
  correlation_id (nil UUID v4), and duration_ms (zero) for reproducible
  evidence snapshots
- Iterates all lines through validate_log_entry_value() to enforce
  E2E schema compliance
- Runs scan_log_jsonl() and asserts zero error count for clean evidence

This brings BigQuery, Bitbucket, and Dropbox compliance tests in line
with the evidence validation pattern already established in the Algolia
and Asana E2E suites, ensuring all connector compliance evidence is
machine-verifiable and audit-ready.

New imports: E2eReport, scan_log_jsonl, validate_log_entry_value from
fcp_e2e crate.

.beads/issues.jsonl: sync issue tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
